brand.name: Coach
brand.note: Brand-level facts assumed consistent with parallel Tabby research per task instructions; not re-verified here.

style.name: Swagger
style.description: The Swagger was introduced for Spring 2015 under creative director Stuart Vevers as part of Coach's broader 'Coach 1941' design-led relaunch, which leaned into the brand's heritage pebbled leather and statement turn-lock hardware after Vevers joined as creative director in 2013. The Swagger Carryall is a structured satchel built around a prominent double turn-lock closure on a leather 'belt' strip across the front flap, marketed as a flagship Vevers-era design alongside other turn-lock styles. Available in multiple sizes (Swagger 20/21/27/Wristlet) and later in colorblock and exotic-embossed leather variants.
sources_for_year: Marie France Asia Spring 2015 Coach handbag collection coverage
The Sweetest Escape blog, Coach Swagger 20 Mini Review (June 2016) describing Swagger as a recent/newest bag

variants.exterior_colorway: varies — common colorways include Black, Oxblood, Saddle/Tan, Metallic Bronze; multiple colorblock and exotic-embossed versions also produced
variants.hardware_color: varies by colorway — light gold and dark gunmetal/nickel most common
variants.hardware_type: double turn-lock (statement belting strap with double turn-lock across front flap)
variants.strap_type: structured top handle (fixed, ~4 inch drop) plus detachable/adjustable crossbody strap (~19 inch drop)
variants.strap_attachment_type: fixed top handle; D-ring/clip attachment for removable crossbody strap
variants.market_availability: US, global retail (Coach boutiques, department stores, Coach Outlet)
variants.retail_price_original_note: Sources disagree: Swagger 21 Carryall MSRP commonly cited at $350; some Swagger 27 resale/retail listings cite $450 original MSRP. Could not verify a single authoritative original Swagger 27 price, so left null. Treat $350-$450 as a plausible range pending better sourcing.
variants.authentication_markers: Pebbled leather should show natural, slightly irregular grain (not a uniform embossed/plastic-like pattern); leather should compress and rebound when pinched near a seam rather than crease sharply. Turn-lock should be substantial, not hollow-feeling, with crisp, evenly engraved 'COACH' lettering — smudged or shallow engraving is a fake indicator. Could not verify Swagger-specific turn-lock weight figures from any source.

production_records.dimensions_note: Converted from official Coach listing: 10 3/4" (L) x 7 3/4" (H) x 5 3/4" (W). L mapped to width, H to height, W to depth.
production_records.date_code_format: Coach moved away from interior creed stamps with printed serial numbers in late 2014. For bags produced from roughly 2015 onward (which includes the entire Swagger production run), the serial/date code typically appears on a small white fabric tag sewn into an interior seam or pocket corner rather than stamped on a leather creed patch. Format generally follows: style number (5-6 digits) + color code (3 digits) + date code (letter-number combination for factory/month/year), e.g. format pattern like 'DS1503' decoding to March 2015, USA factory. This is the general post-2014 Coach convention; not verified specifically against a physical Swagger 27 tag.
production_records.stamp_placement: No leather creed stamp expected for this era; look for small white fabric tag sewn into a bottom interior seam or inside a pocket.
production_records.known_authentication_markers: If a Swagger 27 has a leather creed patch with a stamped serial number in the older LL/DDD/YY format, that would be inconsistent with the bag's 2015+ production era and should raise suspicion. Absence of a leather creed stamp and presence of a small white sewn-in fabric tag is normal and expected for this bag, not a red flag.

known_color_combinations.authentication_notes: Black pebbled leather Swagger with light gold turn-lock is one of the most commonly seen colorways at thrift stores; could not verify an authoritative interior-color pairing.
Oxblood (deep burgundy) pebble leather is a documented retail colorway (e.g. Amazon listing 'Li/Oxblood'). Interior/stitching pairing not verified.

carry_methods.notes: Fixed top handle, approximately 4 inch (10.2cm) drop per official Coach spec, suitable for hand or wrist carry, not crook of arm given short drop.
4 inch handle drop is short for crook-of-arm carry; depends on wearer's arm/hand size. Not independently verified by a fit tester.
Detachable, adjustable crossbody strap with approximately 19 inch (48.3cm) drop per official Coach spec, designed for shoulder/crossbody wear.
Same detachable strap used for shoulder or crossbody carry depending on length adjustment.

fits.notes: Not independently verified by a fit tester; plausible given bag size but no source confirms explicitly.
Not independently verified; could not find a source confirming fit.
Not independently verified; could not find a source confirming fit.

interior_storage.authentication_notes: Official Coach listing confirms 'inside zip and multifunction pockets' but exact count/placement of each pocket type not itemized in source.

interior_storage.placement: interior, multifunction pockets per official description
interior_storage.authentication_notes: Described only as 'multifunction pockets' in official source; exact quantity not specified.

serial_tags.how_to_read: Older format: LL/DDD/YY (two-letter factory/inspector code, style number, year) — this format predates the Swagger's 2015 introduction and would NOT be expected on a genuine Swagger.
serial_tags.authentication_notes: A Swagger 27 with this older creed format and serial style would be inconsistent with its known production era — worth flagging as suspicious, though not independently confirmed against an actual Swagger unit.

serial_tags.format: Style number (5-6 digits) + color code (3 digits) + date code (letter+digits, e.g. 'DS1503' = March 2015, USA factory)
serial_tags.placement: Small white fabric tag sewn into an interior seam or pocket corner
serial_tags.year_range: approx. 2014/2015 onward, which covers the entire known Swagger production run
serial_tags.how_to_read: General post-2014 Coach convention per multiple reseller/authentication guides; not verified against an actual photographed Swagger 27 tag.
serial_tags.authentication_notes: A blank leather creed patch combined with a small white sewn fabric tag is normal and expected for this era — not a fake indicator by itself.

locks_and_keys.lock_type: turn-lock closure only (not a separate padlock/key mechanism)
locks_and_keys.lock_engraving: COACH logo engraved on turn-lock hardware
locks_and_keys.authentication_notes: Swagger's signature hardware is a double turn-lock closure integrated into the front flap design, not a separate detachable padlock with keys. No source found indicating Swagger ever included a lock-and-key set.

provenance_packaging.description: Coach dust bags are commonly included with new handbag purchases across the brand's product line
provenance_packaging.material: fabric (typically a light cotton/flannel-type material per general Coach packaging convention)
provenance_packaging.branding: COACH logo print, per general brand convention
provenance_packaging.authentication_notes: Could not verify Swagger-specific dust bag color/branding details from a dedicated source; this is inferred from general Coach packaging practice, not confirmed for this style.
